tldr: Tom — you've got 3 live ads and 2 of them have been running unchanged for 151 days, which is roughly 5× past where carousels in apparel typically stall out. The bigger issue: at 3 distinct concepts you're sitting under Meta's Andromeda 5-concept floor, which means the algorithm is treating your active set as functionally one ad.

benchmark.context: Most apparel brands at your scale refresh hero creative every ~21 days; the top quartile sits closer to 14. You've got two carousels past the 150-day mark, and only 3 distinct concepts running — Andromeda's retrieval engine (Meta's October 2025 update) needs at least 5 semantically distinct concepts to actually spread delivery, so right now the system is clustering your three ads as one.

ads.headline: Vanderpump Rules It's Not About the Pasta! Men's Tri-Blend Short Sleeve T-Shirt
ads.body: Make sure your love for Bravo is always on display with new apparel and merch from Shop by Bravo.
drivers: 151 days running unchanged — that's more than 5× your typical 28-day refresh window, and well past the 14–21 day window Andromeda rewards.
Headline is the raw product title ('Vanderpump Rules It's Not About the Pasta! Men's Tri-Blend Short Sleeve T-Shirt') — no hook, no promise, just SKU language pulled straight from the catalog.
Body copy ('Make sure your love for Bravo is always on display...') is a near-twin of your Bravoholic sweatshirt ad, so to Andromeda these read as the same concept.

ads.headline: Bravoholic Fleece Crewneck Sweatshirt
ads.body: Check out Shop by Bravo for best-selling merch & apparel from your favorite shows!
drivers: Also at 151 days live — same launch batch as the Vanderpump tee, which is the burst-and-stall pattern (everything ships at once, then nothing for 5 months).
Headline is just the product name — no fan hook, no show callback, no urgency. The Bravoholic identity itself is the strongest emotional handle you have and it's wasted as a SKU label.
'Best-selling merch & apparel from your favorite shows' is generic catalog copy — it doesn't tell a Vanderpump fan, a RHONY fan, or a Below Deck fan that this product is for them specifically.

ads.headline: Shop Bravo x Reebok
ads.body: Step into style with the limited-edition Bravo x Reebok collab!
drivers: 32 days in — just past your 28-day window, which is where carousels in this category usually start to soften, but the collab angle is still the freshest concept in your active set.
'Limited-edition' is doing real work here — it's the only ad with built-in scarcity, and it's the only one not pulling its headline from a product title.

Same carousel format as the other two ads, so even though the angle is fresh, the visual rhythm in feed is identical — a customer scrolling past sees three carousels in a row, not three distinct stories.

hero_concept.headline: For the Bravoholics.
hero_concept.primary_text:
Some shows you watch. Bravo shows you quote at brunch.

The Vanderpump tri-blend tee was made for the people who finish each other's reunion-episode sentences — soft hand, vintage feel, the kind of shirt you wear once and then keep stealing back from the laundry pile.

New drops every month at Shop by Bravo. Free shipping on orders over $50.
hero_concept.visual_direction: Editorial flat-lay of the tri-blend tee on a warm neutral surface with a coffee mug, sunglasses, and a folded magazine — lifestyle context that reads 'Sunday brunch with the group chat' rather than catalog product shot.
hero_concept.fills_gap: Every one of your live ads is a carousel using the raw product title as the headline — there's no static format and no fan-identity hook in the active set. Andromeda needs a different visual rhythm and a distinct emotional angle to register this as a new concept, not a fourth variant of the same idea.
hero_concept.image_prompt: A photorealistic editorial product photograph featuring the product from the reference image, styled in a warm neutral flat-lay scene on a soft cream linen surface with a stoneware coffee mug, folded tortoiseshell sunglasses, and a half-folded magazine arranged loosely beside the tee, with morning light raking across the composition. Square 1:1 composition with breathing room top and bottom for ad text overlay. CRITICAL — preserve the exact heathered tri-blend fabric texture and the exact colorway of the tee shown in the reference (do NOT shift to solid cotton, do NOT change the heather tone, do NOT alter saturation), AND preserve every printed graphic, text element, illustration, and color on the front of the tee exactly as shown — strictly NO color shift, NO removing of print, NO altering of graphic colors or typography. STRUCTURE — preserve the exact construction: crew neckline, short sleeves, regular men's straight fit, printed graphic centered on the chest exactly as shown including all text and any illustration elements, printed brand label at the back of the neck if visible. PRESERVE the front-chest printed graphic exactly — every word, every illustration, all colors, same placement and scale. Do NOT remove the print, do NOT alter the graphic text or wording, do NOT change neckline or sleeve length, do NOT add pockets, do NOT change to a v-neck or scoop neck, do NOT alter the fit silhouette. Render the product's fabric, weave, color, finish, AND every visible construction detail (silhouette, neckline, sleeves, printed graphics) EXACTLY as they appear in the reference image. Do NOT change the color temperature, do NOT introduce tones not present in the reference, do NOT add or remove any hardware, do NOT alter the silhouette, do NOT remove or modify printed graphics or text on the garment. IMPORTANT: the 'no text' and 'no logos' items in the NEGATIVE list below apply ONLY to text or logos added as overlay to the photograph itself — text, logos, graphics, prints, and brand labels that exist ON the product in the reference image are part of the product and must be preserved exactly. Premium commerce photography quality. NEGATIVE: no overlay text added to the photograph, no overlay logos added to the photograph, no watermarks, no UI elements, no Facebook interface, no clickable buttons, no faces, no models, no collage, no borders, no duplicate products, no color shift, no metal swap, no silver-to-gold or gold-to-silver conversion, no warm-tone bias on cool metals, no recoloring of fabric or stones, no pattern modifications, no removing or replacing the product's printed graphics, no extra hardware not present in reference, no added pockets or pleats, no silhouette alterations, no design modifications.
hero_concept.reference_title: BRAVO Boy Dress Shoe WILLIAM-4KID Loafer Shoe School Uniform Brown

next_step.urgency: Two of your three ads are 151 days old — they're past the point where minor edits will reset delivery.
next_step.headline: Walk through the Bravo Shoes refresh plan with Kyle
next_step.body:
Tom, in this 30 min call, we'll map out:
• 3 customer avatars at your price point
• The motivational hook driving each
• A 30-day creative + spend roadmap (to outbeat your current ROAS)

Yours to keep — work with us or don't.

method_note: Built from a manual walk-through of every ad currently running on Bravo's Meta Ad Library — no account access, no private metrics, just public creative signal mapped against 2025–2026 Meta platform reality.
